author: @zylascope
text: So are the resin prints stronger than the filament type I wonder? It does look smoother from what I can tell. Thanks Luke. The production quality of your videos keeps improving all the time. Good lighting, good sound, clear speaking, good audio levels, no glitches, good transitions/edits, great content.

author: @scotttovey
text:
Unless they've improved the technology, you will want to dip or spray that cage with a clear coat UV blocker or paint.

I read some time ago that objects which are made with UV curable resins and plastics continue to cure in the sunlight and eventually become very brittle and break.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you for the suggestion mate! I never thought of that, so I will give them a clear coat before I paint them. :)

replies.author: @scotttovey

author: @William_Hada
text: Congrats on your first print! Impressive! It's seems that resin printed objects need to be printed at an angle. Why is that? Just to reduce the height/length on the  printer plate?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you mate! It's because the ring would not have fit on the plate while printing, so I had to put them at a weird angle so they could print lol! :)

author: @barbufodor1186
text: Congrats! Now you are hooked. You'll look for reasons to use the printer. It is like drugs ... I went in vacation  and suffered withdrawal symptoms.

author: @dgpreston5593
text:
Good job, Luke!
I can't say my first-ever 3D print job came out that well.  😆

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you mate! I really thought something would of went wrong LOL! :P
